conversations.content: Let's do an Azeroth etiquette quiz to test your understanding of the etiquette rules.

Answer using the Azeroth world etiquette rules mentioned the day before yesterday: On the Isle of Quel'Danas, what action must you absolutely never make when asking a Blood Elf Magister for magical advice?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Taboo actions when asking a Blood Elf Magister for magical advice under the Azeroth world etiquette rules mentioned the day before yesterday</content>

Related memory fragments: [mem-id:535] Time: 2025-08-04-10:10, Content: The user taught the taboos of communicating with Blood Elf Magisters on the Isle of Quel'Danas, Azeroth. The core is to absolutely never point a finger directly at the Magister's staff, crystal, or grimoire, nor make any casting gestures (even if just a habitual gesture), otherwise it will be considered a desecration of magical power. Applicable to the vicinity of the Sunwell Plateau and inside the Magisters' Terrace. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in memory, [mem-id:535] explicitly states not to point at the staff, crystal, or grimoire, and not to make casting gestures.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth world etiquette rules, when asking a Blood Elf Magister for magical advice, you must absolutely never point directly at their staff, crystal, or grimoire with your hand, nor make any casting gestures.
Correct! You have mastered this etiquette rule.
